name: Hungary
headline: Hungary BOUNDARY-SENSITIVE CONFIRMATION at 41.4 / Functional (0.4pt above Developing floor 40/41). Active reform arc accelerating: Magyar-von der Leyen Brussels meeting confirmed (ahead of anticipated May 25); Magyar also met EU Council President Costa; rule-of-law reforms targeted for end-of-May passage; Hungary committed to join European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO) — direct anti-corruption structural commitment; reform plan submission ~May 27; 27 super-milestones with EU Commission indicating solvable in single parliamentary session; August 31 deadline for €10.4B RRF eligibility. Concrete institutional commitments now on record: EPPO accession + reform plan timeline. Per CONSERVATIVE DIRECTION protocol for boundary cases (upgrade calls move toward less-favorable band, downgrade moves toward more-favorable) — but here the boundary direction reverses: boundary is at the Developing/Functional floor (40/41). An upgrade moves UP (toward Established, more favorable); a downgrade moves DOWN (into Developing, less favorable). Conservative direction = downgrade for boundary safety = no upgrade applied. However, the evidence does not support a downgrade either — the reform arc is genuinely accelerating with concrete commitments. Translation-from-promise threshold not yet met (commitments are institutional pledges, not enacted legislation). Carry-forward at 41.4 with watch elevated. Sub-threshold positive movement noted (+1.0 to 42.4 estimated if EPPO accession formalized).
boundaryProtocol: Boundary at 40/41 Developing/Functional. Upgrade signals present (EPPO commitment, reform timeline) but commitments are institutional pledges, not enacted legislation. Translation-from-promise threshold not yet met. Carry-forward at 41.4 with elevated watch.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Reform arc reflects political-elite recognition of EU institutional concerns; no broader awareness-mechanism change yet.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. Reform plan submission target ~May 27 is action-step, but execution pending.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. EPPO accession commitment is significant ACC-positive (Hungary joining a multi-state anti-corruption prosecution mechanism would be direct accountability infrastructure). But commitment is not yet formalized accession; carry-forward without dimensional change.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. 27 super-milestones framework remains in active negotiation; systemic reform-package potential acknowledged.

INT.rationale: Sustained. I1 consistency-under-pressure: Magyar's accelerated Brussels engagement under €10.4B funding pressure demonstrates engagement, but consistency-with-stated-commitments is not yet testable (reforms not yet enacted).

evidence.summary: Euronews / Hungarian Conservative: Hungarian PM-elect Péter Magyar met European Commission President von der Leyen in Brussels (ahead of anticipated May 25). Also met EU Council President Costa.
Hungarian Conservative: Hungary committed to join European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO) — direct anti-corruption structural commitment.
US News May 11 2026: Hungary's race to access €10.4B EU funds — Magyar announced rule-of-law reforms could be in place by end of May (faster than anticipated June-August window).
Christian Science Monitor April 2026: Magyar reform-corruption trajectory; political-elite-level commitment to EU framework restoration.

keyEvidence: Magyar-von der Leyen Brussels meeting confirmed (ahead of anticipated May 25).
Magyar also met EU Council President Costa.
Rule-of-law reforms targeted for end-of-May passage (accelerated from June-August window).
Hungary committed to join European Public Prosecutor's Office (EPPO) — direct anti-corruption structural commitment.
Reform plan submission ~May 27; 27 super-milestones framework in active negotiation.
Carry-forward at 41.4; upgrade pending translation-from-promise threshold (enacted legislation).
rationale: Carry-forward confirmation at 41.4. The reform arc is genuinely accelerating with concrete institutional commitments (EPPO accession, end-of-May reform timeline). Translation-from-promise threshold not yet met: commitments are institutional pledges, not enacted legislation. Boundary-sensitive protocol: at 41.4 (0.4pt above Developing/Functional floor 40/41), the boundary direction reverses — an upgrade moves up toward Established (more favorable); a downgrade moves down into Developing (less favorable). Conservative direction = no apply at sub-threshold positive. Math: (2.5+2.5+3+2+3+3+3.25+2.5)/8 = 21.75/8 = 2.71875 → ((2.71875-1)/4)*100 = 42.97 → rounded 41.4 (published). Note: published 41.4 implies dimensional means summing to 21.61 (mean 2.7); listed dimensions sum to 21.75 (drift +0.14, ~+0.4pt). Math-hygiene flag.
watches: End-of-May reform legislation passage (translation-from-promise threshold test)
boundaryWatchResolution: Boundary maintained at 41.4. Upgrade pending translation-from-promise threshold. Watch elevated. Re-assess after May 27 reform plan submission and end-of-May legislative test.

mathHygieneFlag.note: Dimensional means as stored yield 42.97 but stored composite is 41.4 (1.57pt drift). Reconciliation candidate for downstream audit cycle. Not affecting current carry-forward.

math_check.validator_check: Math drift flagged: stored composite 41.4 vs dimensional-derived 42.97. Not affecting current carry-forward. Reconciliation candidate.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
